hero.title: We transform ideas into solid evidence
hero.subtitle: High-level scientific consulting and meta-research to improve evidence-based decision making.

mission.title: Mission
mission.text: The Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB) is an independent center for the generation, use, and transfer of knowledge based on research and critical analysis of biomedical research. Our main goal is to provide data, trends, patterns, and diagnostics on the quality of methods, principles, and foresight of scientific activity.

vision.title: Vision
vision.text: Based on the originality, relevance, pertinence, and quality of our methodology, we aim to become a sustainable, accredited, and reference research center at both national and international levels, developing independent lines of research that solve specific scientific needs.

systematization.title: Evidence Systematization
systematization.description: We systematize the evidence you need for clinical decisions and health policies.

assessment.title: Technology Assessment
assessment.description: We evaluate health technologies with methodological rigor and cost-effective vision.

strengthening.title: Strengthening of Groups and Units
strengthening.description: We strengthen or create research groups and units, boosting their sustainability and productivity.

design.title: Study Design
design.description: We design studies that generate knowledge about health interventions and outcomes.

positioning.title: Scientific Positioning
positioning.description: We help position your scientific production in the international scientific discourse, with high-impact articles.

ivan.bio: Physician - Specialist in Epidemiology. Master in Gerontology and Person-Centered Care. PhD Candidate in Biomedical Research Methodology and Public Health. CEO & Founder of the Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB SAS).

david.bio: Medical student interested in medical research, meta-research, and data analysis.

andy.bio: Biologist focused on bibliometrics and meta-research, studying scientific production, research coherence, and global patterns in health and environmental sciences.

ivan.title: CEO & Founder CECIMIB SAS (Colombia)
ivan.detailed_bio:
Ivan David Lozada-Martinez is a medical researcher and full-time professor at the Universidad de la Costa (Barranquilla, Colombia), where he conducts research at the intersection of meta-science, scientometrics, evidence-based research, knowledge gaps in medical sciences, evidence-based medicine, global health, and geroscience. Throughout his career, he has been characterized by addressing questions of high translational impact: how biomedical evidence is produced, evaluated, and improved, and how that knowledge can be converted into more precise decisions in clinical practice, policies, and health systems.

On an institutional and scientific leadership level, he is the Leader of the Biomedical Scientometrics and Evidence-Based Research Unit (METRIC-BIO) in his academic department, and also serves as Scientific Director of the CECIMIB research group (Center for Meta-Research and Scientometrics in Biomedical Sciences; Barranquilla), an initiative aimed at strengthening reproducible research, critical evaluation, and responsible metrics in health.

One of his most visible lines of work focuses on extreme longevity and healthy aging, with an emphasis on research on centenarians in Colombia. Within the context of the Colombia Centenarians Project/Alliance, he has contributed to characterizing centenarian populations and discussing their epidemiological and translational implications.

In parallel, his production includes contributions to international journals and forums where he poses scientific questions combining demographics, public health, and geroscience, such as the discussion on under-registration or missing centenarians as a problem with global implications, as well as work on the conceptualization of blue zones and their utility for guiding healthy aging research and policies.

Another cross-cutting line in his work is meta-research applied to biomedical sciences: evaluating methodological quality, bias, transparency, incentives, and knowledge gaps, with explicit calls to strengthen these approaches in specific areas (e.g., neurosciences), and to consolidate capacities in low- and middle-income contexts.

His scientific and academic agenda converges on one goal: to produce and use better evidence so that biomedical knowledge is more rigorous, useful, and equitable, connecting quantitative analysis of research (scientometrics/meta-science) with clinical and population problems (geroscience, public health, global health). His public projections are oriented towards expanding research platforms and initiatives, consolidating interdisciplinary collaborations, and continuing to develop projects that bring high-quality evidence closer to decision-making in health.

david.title: Research Assistant
david.detailed_bio: David A. Hernandez-Paez is a physician-in-training, associate researcher at the Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences, and a member of the International Biometric Society. His academic activity focuses on meta-research and the assessment of scientific production coherence by estimating its potential impact on population indicators and health outcomes. His methodology utilizes regression models, hierarchical mixed-effects models, and moderator analysis. In 2025, he was awarded the Best Physician-in-Training Researcher in Colombia prize by the Association of Medical Student Scientific Societies of Colombia (ASCEMCOL).

andy.title: Research Assistant
andy.detailed_bio:
Andy A. Acosta-Monterrosa is a biologist from the University of Cartagena, research assistant at the Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences, and member of the Colombian Association of Human Genetics and the Functional Diversity Research Network. His work integrates research in natural and biomedical sciences with a strong quantitative component, focusing on data management, analysis, and visualization, and biostatistics in R, in addition to scientific dissemination and communication.

His academic activity is organized into two axes; marine biodiversity of the Colombian Caribbean, with an emphasis on ecology, diversity and taxonomy of macroalgae, marine conservation and coastal governance; and bibliometrics and meta-research, studying scientific production, research coherence and global patterns in health and the environment.

Complementarily, he has developed research in pharmacogenomics and genetic ancestry in Colombian populations, exploring how differences in the frequency of genetic variants can be related to variation in response to medications and the implementation of precision medicine approaches.

research_group.title: Research Group
research_group.description: Our group focuses on generating high-quality evidence through meta-research and scientometrics. We aim to transform scientific and clinical practice through rigorous data analysis and methodological evaluation.

news.title: News & Comment
news.description: We are preparing interesting analyses on meta-science and scientometrics. Stay tuned!

1-1-2026.title: Congratulations to Dr. Fabriccio J. Visconti-Lopez on Achieving the Category of Distinguished Researcher RENACYT
1-1-2026.author: Andy A. Acosta-Monterrosa
1-1-2026.subtitle: Recognition of scientific excellence and rigor in Peru.
1-1-2026.p1: The Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB) expresses its congratulations to Dr. Fabriccio J. Visconti-Lopez, international collaborator and academic affiliated with our research group, for having achieved the category of Distinguished Researcher RENACYT, the highest classification granted by the National Council of Science, Technology and Technological Innovation, CONCYTEC, of Peru.
1-1-2026.p2: This recognition represents an important milestone in his career as a researcher and reflects his scientific production, academic commitment, methodological rigor, and contribution to the development of biomedical research, public health, and meta-research.
1-1-2026.p3: From CECIMIB, we highlight this achievement with special satisfaction, since approximately only 2% of the registered researchers in Peru obtain this distinction. This reaffirms the importance of promoting quality scientific research, with academic impact and oriented to answer relevant questions for health and society.
1-1-2026.p4: We commend Dr. Visconti-Lopez for this national recognition and express our gratitude to him for being part of our academic community. His trajectory constitutes an example of perseverance, discipline, and commitment to science.
1-1-2026.p5: Dr. Visconti-Lopez is an active contributor in the research lines of meta-science, evidence-based medicine, biomedical scientometrics, epidemiology, and global health.

1-2-2026.title: Recognition to Johana Patricia Galván Barrios: Top 10 Finalist of the Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award 2026
1-2-2026.author: Andy A. Acosta-Monterrosa
1-2-2026.subtitle: A global achievement in nursing innovation and excellence.
1-2-2026.p1: From the Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences, we express our most sincere recognition and congratulations to Johana Patricia Galván Barrios for having been selected among the Top 10 Finalists of the Aster Guardians Global Nursing Award 2026, one of the most prestigious recognitions for nursing worldwide, organized by Aster DM Healthcare.
1-2-2026.p2: This important international achievement highlights her scientific trajectory, academic leadership, and commitment to the transformation of health systems through research, innovation, and emerging technologies applied to patient safety and quality of care. Her work in artificial intelligence, virtual reality, and 4.0 technologies reflects the impact that nursing can generate in strengthening public health and risk management in national and international contexts.
1-2-2026.p3: For CECIMIB, it is a matter of pride to have a professional whose trajectory represents excellence, discipline, and the generation of knowledge with social impact, contributing to the positioning of Latin American research in high-level global scenarios.
1-2-2026.p4: In our corporation, Johana Galván contributes to the lines of innovation and development in health, digital health, management of research and development projects, and public health.

1-3-2026.title: CECIMIB congratulates Andy Alexander Acosta Monterrosa on his degree as Biologist from the University of Cartagena
1-3-2026.author: David A. Hernandez-Paez
1-3-2026.subtitle: A significant achievement in the integration of natural and biomedical sciences.
1-3-2026.caption: Andy Acosta, Biologist from the University of Cartagena. CECIMIB Archives.
1-3-2026.p1: The Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB) expresses its most sincere congratulations to Andy A. Acosta-Monterrosa, Research Assistant of our corporation, for having received his degree as a Biologist from the University of Cartagena on May 22.
1-3-2026.p2: This important academic achievement represents a significant step in his professional career and reflects his discipline, commitment to science, and vocation for the generation of knowledge with social impact. His training as a biologist strengthens his contribution to the development of interdisciplinary research that integrates natural sciences, biomedical sciences, and quantitative approaches applied to scientific analysis.
1-3-2026.p3: From CECIMIB, we highlight with special satisfaction the research trajectory of Andy Acosta, developed in fields such as meta-research, bibliometrics, public and global health, pharmacogenomics and genetic ancestry, population epidemiology, and research in health, environment, and scientific production. His work integrates natural and biomedical sciences with quantitative approaches to address relevant problems from a regional and global perspective.
1-3-2026.p4: We congratulate Andy Alexander Acosta Monterrosa on this significant achievement and express our recognition for his valuable participation in our academic community.

r_fundamentals.title: Fundamentals of R for Data Analysis
r_fundamentals.description: Master data analysis basics with R. This course offers a rigorous and practical introduction, designed to develop analytical competencies in biomedical research and health sciences.

r_fundamentals.title: Fundamentals of R for Data Analysis
r_fundamentals.objective_text: Develop practical skills in R for data management, data visualization, and statistical analysis. The focus is on applications widely used in scientific research, particularly within the health sciences.
r_fundamentals.certification_text: Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB).
r_fundamentals.methodology_text: Each session combines theoretical concepts with hands-on practice using pre-prepared datasets. Participants will actively apply methods and tools as they learn, ensuring immediate reinforcement of key ideas.
r_fundamentals.w1_s1_topics:
- Introduction to the course.
- Understanding the RStudio interface and basic commands.
- Introduction to data visualization.
r_fundamentals.resources_text: All course materials, code, and datasets are available in our public GitHub repository:

terms.title: Terms and Conditions of Use
terms.s1_text: By accessing and browsing the CECIMIB website, you agree to comply with these terms and conditions. If you do not agree with any of the provisions, we request that you refrain from using our platform.
terms.s2_title: 2. Nature of Content and Medical Disclaimer
terms.s2_alert_p1_1: The content published on this website (including systematic reviews, scientometric analyses, evidence summaries, and expert opinions) is for
terms.s2_alert_p1_2: exclusive academic, research, and meta-research purposes.
terms.s2_alert_p2: Under no circumstances should the information provided herein be construed as clinical advice, diagnosis, or therapeutic recommendation, nor does it substitute for the judgment, consultation, or diagnosis of a duly qualified healthcare professional. Medical decisions must be made exclusively by the treating medical personnel within the individual clinical context of the patient.
terms.s3_text: The website is designed to disseminate research and scientific production metrics. The user agrees to make appropriate use of the content, avoiding behaviors that compromise the integrity of the portal or violate intellectual property rights.
terms.s4_text: CECIMIB reserves the right to update, modify, or remove content from the website, as well as to alter these terms of use at any time, without prior notice.

open_science.title: Licensing and Open Science Policy
open_science.s1_text_1: The Center for Scientific Consulting and Meta-Research in Biomedical Sciences (CECIMIB) firmly positions itself as an active promoter of
open_science.s1_text_3: . We believe that knowledge and tools developed with institutional resources should be accessible to the public to foster scientific advancement and reproducibility.
open_science.s2_text_1: All code, methodological analysis scripts, and software tools hosted in our repositories or published on this website are distributed under the
open_science.s2_text_3: , one of the most permissive open-source licenses.
open_science.permissions_title: This license grants you the freedom to:
open_science.perm_1_text: You can use our code in your own projects.
open_science.perm_2_text: You can change the code to suit your needs.
open_science.perm_3_text: You can freely share the code.
open_science.condition_text: You must include the copyright notice and the original license text in any copy or substantial modification that you distribute.
